global_alarm_phases.behavior: hold forts, run patrols, probe central road lightly, do not attack Carthaginian base
global_alarm_phases.name: watchful

global_alarm_phases.behavior: reinforce the nearest crossing and activate route-specific response
global_alarm_phases.name: alarm
global_alarm_phases.starts_when: player attacks Roman unit, crosses any Rhone bridge, conquers Gaul, or reaches x >= 100

global_alarm_phases.behavior: remaining fort gains emergency troops and Rome launches a bridgehead counterattack
global_alarm_phases.name: one_fort_lost
global_alarm_phases.starts_when: either Roman barracks is captured or destroyed

global_alarm_phases.behavior: final defensive wave; healers stay protected; cavalry targets siege and healers
global_alarm_phases.name: last_stand
global_alarm_phases.starts_when: remaining Roman barracks below 50 percent health or player controls both eastern_island_zone and one fort zone

bridges.role: central island to southern fort connector
central island to northern fort connector

bridges.purpose: gives skilled players a vision route, not a full army shortcut
bridges.role: scout-only visual route; too narrow for siege and elephants

conditions.message: Rome has cut the bridgehead and scattered the crossing force.

description: Hannibal's Carthaginian force begins on the western bank and must break through a layered Roman river defense. The level is built around three viable crossings, a neutral Gallic faction, maze-like approach paths, meaningful route order, asymmetric Roman forts, active patrols, and a supply-line counterattack after the first fort falls.
design_pillars: Every road leads to a readable tactical destination: crossing, hill, village, ambush pocket, cache, or fort.
The west bank is a preparation maze: ridges, ruins, Gallic camps, and scout paths create route selection before the river fight.
The east bank is a pressure maze: Roman forts are linked by patrol roads, island high ground, branch bridges, supply depots, and counterattack lanes.
The player has three primary plans: fast northern strike, brutal central control, or slower southern alliance.
Capturing the first Roman fort changes the second half of the level through reinforcements, bridge raids, and supply-line pressure.

events.message: The Rhone blocks us. The north road is fast but exposed, the center is heavily held, and the southern Gauls may be persuaded before we cross.
Our scouts find abandoned supplies and a view toward a narrow plank crossing.
Roman horns answer from the far bank. They know where we are crossing.
The watch hill is ours. Roman patrols on the east bank are revealed for a short time.
The island road is under our control. Roman movement between the forts slows.
The northern command post has fallen. The southern fort closes ranks and prepares a heavy counterattack.
The southern military fort has fallen. The northern commander signals cavalry reserves and brings the catapult to bear.
Rome is moving to cut our bridgehead. Hold the crossing or finish the assault quickly.
The last Roman fort commits everything. Break them now.

eastern_bank_branches.danger: army can be split and counterattacked
eastern_bank_branches.leads_to: slower Roman patrols and choice of north/south attack

maze_readability.principle: The maze should be tactical, not confusing. Every branch has a visible lure and a consequence.

western_bank_branches.leads_to: hidden plank, signal tower view, north rush information
western_bank_branches.lure: broken cart and scout cache at [45,26]

western_bank_branches.danger: Roman scout loop and exposed approach
western_bank_branches.lure: burnt cart and narrow road toward [89,55]

western_bank_branches.danger: time delay and southern cavalry probe
western_bank_branches.lure: Gallic campfires and guide horse at [82,116]

intended_difficulty_notes: The player should feel overextended after the first fort, not finished.
No road is decorative-only; each has a destination, reward, or tactical information purpose.
Hills break sightlines and create staging pockets on both banks.
Props mark gameplay affordances: carts for supplies/routes, weapon racks for military zones, watch fires for vision, signal tower for command, siege cache for reward.
The maze must allow planning: branches are visible and themed, not random dead ends.

name: Crossing the Rhone - Polished Maze Version

patrols.purpose: shows the north route and can alarm the fort if ignored
keeps east bank alive and punishes unsupported split pushes
creates timing windows for scouting and attack
pressures southern bridge without fully blocking it
visually teaches the southern alliance route
uses the far-eastern prop lane and prevents edge of map from feeling dead

prop_clusters.gameplay_role: draws light units toward hidden plank

rewards.fantasy: The command fort falls; Roman coordination weakens.
The military fort falls; its siege stores become Carthaginian weapons.

rivers.role: primary map divider and commitment line
separates the southern fort from the central island
forces the northern route to pass through branch-bridge decisions

roads.purpose: main readable road to the hardest crossing
long safe route toward Gaul and southern crossing
fast but narrow route for cavalry and early pressure
optional scout route ending in vision, not a full army attack path
connects northern route to neutral Gallic warning camp
low-risk scouting path from player camp to southern route
makes the Gallic objective clearly feed into the southern plan
lets Gaul alliance support a central push instead of only south
immediate tactical objective after central crossing
connects central control route to southern fort
connects central control route to northern fort
scout/raid route to signal rider and tower
central route into southern military fort
Roman patrol road linking both objectives
inner Roman response road from island to command fort
visual Roman fallback lane; used by patrols and final cavalry wave

route_design.intended_choice: Use cavalry and elephant pressure to dive the northern command fort before southern Rome is fully ready.
route_design.path_shape: thin road through broken woodland, then exposed hill road into the northern command fort
route_design.reward: early capture weakens Roman scouting, slows fort-to-fort patrols, and gives vision over the northern road
route_design.risk: narrow bridge, exposed road, Roman archers on high ground, catapult threatens clumped infantry

route_design.intended_choice: Accept the hardest first fight to own the central island and force Rome to defend both forts at once.
route_design.path_shape: wide road into a fortified watch hill, then choice of north branch or south branch
route_design.reward: captured watch hill gives patrol vision and early warning for bridge counterattacks
route_design.risk: highest initial casualties; Roman watch hill, spear wall, archers, and rotating patrols overlap here

route_design.intended_choice: Secure Gallic help, then pressure the southern military fort with a bigger army.
route_design.path_shape: long route through Gallic village, ravines, and low ridges before crossing near the southern fort
route_design.reward: Gauls can add units, reveal traps, or give a temporary anti-cavalry advantage
route_design.risk: takes longer, gives Rome fortification time, and leaves the northern catapult/scout network intact

route_design.intended_choice: Delay crossing to gather information and supplies.
route_design.path_shape: looping west-bank ridge paths connecting ruins, caches, and Gallic scouts
route_design.reward: find food, wood, healers, and hints about Roman patrol timing
route_design.risk: Roman alarm timer advances if the player spends too long or attacks Gauls

choices.condition: Enter gaul_village_zone without attacking Gaul and hold nearby for 45 seconds
choices.tradeoff: Rome begins fortifying after the delay

choices.result: Gain population and resources but lose alliance reward
choices.tradeoff: Roman alarm immediately moves to phase 2 and Gallic healer is lost

choices.condition: Cross any Rhone bridge before resolving Gaul
choices.tradeoff: Faster crossing, but less resilience after first Roman counterattack

side_objectives.design_role: Turns the south-west road into a major strategic branch instead of a dead road.
side_objectives.title: Deal with the Gauls

side_objectives.reward: Reveal Roman connector patrols and bridge raid warnings for 180 seconds
side_objectives.title: Seize the Watch Hill
side_objectives.tradeoff: Requires fighting the strongest central defense

side_objectives.condition: Kill rome_north_signal_guard_01 or destroy signal_tower prop before the first Roman fort falls
side_objectives.reward: The second Roman fort receives fewer emergency reinforcements
side_objectives.title: Silence the Signal Tower

side_objectives.condition: Bring a light unit to north_scout_cache_zone
side_objectives.title: Recover the Abandoned Scout Cache

side_objectives.reward: Roman fort connector patrol interval increases by 40 percent
side_objectives.title: Secure the Eastern Island
side_objectives.tradeoff: Holding the island splits the army between both Roman forts

spawns.role: runs warning to south if not intercepted
draws attention to southern alliance route

terrain.description: Player staging ridge overlooking the Carthaginian camp; safe high ground for learning army composition.
Thin scout ridge above the hidden plank route; not suitable for siege.
Low broken woodland hill splitting the camp-to-north route into two sightlines.
Central ridge splitting the southern and northern approach roads; creates maze choice before the river.
Low basin with ruins and grass that visually separates central and southern roads.
Long broken ridge forming the southern preparation maze and protecting the Gallic road.
River overlook above the central bridge; player can stage archers here before committing.
Ridge overlooking the north bridge; a staging choice for rush players but vulnerable to Roman catapult fire.
Hill above the southern bridge that allows allied Gaul archers to matter.
Large Gallic ridge with two entrances; supports diplomacy, conquest, or bypass.
Small sacred grove mound behind the Gallic village; prop-heavy landmark and optional healer reward.
Key Roman watch hill guarding central crossing; capture reveals eastern patrols.
Island high ground that controls movement between north and south forts; central prize after crossing.
Southern mound supporting the military fort; strong infantry defensive position.
Back hill behind the southern Roman fort; creates archer line and prevents the base from feeling flat.
Northern command fort hill; supports archers, catapult, and signal tower.
Outer saddle near the north fort; Roman cavalry rotates through it, but players can flank it from the scout plank.
Cliff above the east branch, compressing movement near the branch bridge.
Northwestern rocky boundary
Northern valley wall
Southwestern rocky boundary
Southern valley wall
Far eastern ridge and visual boundary

victory.description: Force the Rhone crossing and capture both Roman barracks at the northern command fort and southern military fort.

world_props.purpose: north command identity and optional objective

zones.purpose: north command objective and alarm reducer
